title: thalictrum flavum subsp. glaucum
intro: Thalictrum flavum subsp. glaucum, also known as the glaucous meadow rue, is a perennial plant species in the family Ranunculaceae. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Thalictrum flavum subsp. glaucum include treating conditions such as bronchitis, colds, and fever, as well as providing relief from skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
